gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Extract the substring of `s` given by the byte range `[start, end)`, returning a new `Text`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The range must:"] # [doc = " - have `start <= end <= s.len_bytes()` (otherwise `Err(OutOfRange)`), and"] # [doc = " - have both `start` and `end` on char boundaries (otherwise `Err(NotCharBoundary)`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (when `Ok`)"] # [doc = " The returned slice is exactly the bytes of `s` in the range; no approximation or truncation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Err(BoundaryError::OutOfRange { len, asked })` — range end or start is past the end."] # [doc = " - `Err(BoundaryError::InvalidRange { start, end })` — `start > end` (inverted range)."] # [doc = " - `Err(BoundaryError::NotCharBoundary { byte })` — `start` or `end` is inside a codepoint."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never a silent truncation to the nearest boundary** (C1 / G2), and **never a runtime"] # [doc = " slice-index panic** on an inverted range."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] pub fn slice (s : & Text , start : usize , end : usize) -> Result < Text , BoundaryError > { let len = s . len_bytes () ; if start > len { return Err (BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { len , asked : start }) ; } if end > len { return Err (BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { len , asked : end }) ; } if start > end { return Err (BoundaryError :: InvalidRange { start , end }) ; } let raw = s . as_str () ; if ! raw . is_char_boundary (start) { return Err (BoundaryError :: NotCharBoundary { byte : start }) ; } if ! raw . is_char_boundary (end) { return Err (BoundaryError :: NotCharBoundary { byte : end }) ; } Ok (Text :: new (& raw [start .. end])) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Parse a decimal integer from `s` (fallible — `Result`, **never a sentinel**)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Accepts an optional leading `-` for negative values. Whitespace is not stripped; the caller"] # [doc = " must trim first if needed (no silent trimming — C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (when `Ok`)"] # [doc = " When `Ok`, the result is exactly the integer the string denotes."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Err(ParseErr::Empty)` — `s` is empty."] # [doc = " - `Err(ParseErr::Invalid { at, expected, found })` — a non-digit / non-sign character was"] # [doc = "   found at byte `at`; `expected` names what was expected; `found` carries the offending char."] # [doc = " - `Err(ParseErr::OutOfRange { at, target })` — the digit sequence is valid but exceeds the"] # [doc = "   `i64` range (FLAG Q3 — value-range failure is the numerics module's; `text` hands off here)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never a sentinel `0`** (C1 / G2 / RFC-0016 §4.4 honesty crux)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q3 (parse ↔ numerics value-semantics seam)"] # [doc = " `parse_int` returns `i64` as a placeholder. The authoritative numeric type and its range"] # [doc = " semantics live in `math`/`numerics` (M-525/M-512). The `OutOfRange` variant is the hand-off"] # [doc = " to the numeric module; resolution of the exact type signature is deferred (spec §7-Q3)."] pub fn parse_int (s : & Text) -> Result < i64 , ParseErr > { let raw = s . as_str () ; if raw . is_empty () { return Err (ParseErr :: Empty) ; } let mut iter = raw . char_indices () . peekable () ; let first = iter . next () . expect ("non-empty") ; if first . 1 == '-' || first . 1 == '+' { if iter . peek () . is_none () { return Err (ParseErr :: Invalid { at : first . 0 , expected : "digit after sign" , found : first . 1 . to_string () , }) ; } } else if ! first . 1 . is_ascii_digit () { return Err (ParseErr :: Invalid { at : first . 0 , expected : "digit or sign" , found : first . 1 . to_string () , }) ; } for (i , ch) in iter { if ! ch . is_ascii_digit () { return Err (ParseErr :: Invalid { at : i , expected : "digit" , found : ch . to_string () , }) ; } } raw . parse :: < i64 > () . map_err (| _ | ParseErr :: OutOfRange { at : 0 , target : "i64" , }) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Encode `s` in Latin-1 (ISO-8859-1), strict — `Err` on any non-Latin-1 character."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Latin-1 can represent U+0000–U+00FF. Any character with a codepoint above U+00FF returns"] # [doc = " `Err(EncodeError::Unrepresentable { ch, at, target_encoding })`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (when `Ok`)"] # [doc = " When `Ok`, each byte is exactly the Latin-1 code unit for the corresponding character."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `Err(EncodeError::Unrepresentable { ch, at, target_encoding })`"] # [doc = " **Never a silent U+FFFD substitution** (C1 / G2). The error carries the unrepresentable"] # [doc = " character, its char-boundary index in `s`, and `\"Latin-1\"` as the target encoding."] # [doc = " A caller who wants substitution must call [`to_latin1_lossy`] explicitly."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] pub fn to_latin1 (s : & Text) -> Result < Vec < u8 > , EncodeError > { let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (s . len_bytes ()) ; for (char_idx , ch) in s . as_str () . chars () . enumerate () { let cp = ch as u32 ; if cp > 0xFF { return Err (EncodeError :: Unrepresentable { ch , at : char_idx , target_encoding : "Latin-1" , }) ; } out . push (cp as u8) ; } Ok (out) }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `Vec :: with_capacity` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Encode `s` in Latin-1, substituting non-Latin-1 characters with `marker` (opt-in lossy)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the **distinct, named** opt-in to lossy encoding (spec §3 / C1 / G2). A caller cannot"] # [doc = " get U+FFFD substitution silently — they must call this op explicitly. The `Lossy<Vec<u8>>`"] # [doc = " return type carries the substitution count in the value: the lossiness is **un-droppable**."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` / total"] # [doc = " Total — every character either encodes to its Latin-1 byte or is substituted with `marker`."] # [doc = " The `substituted` count in the `Lossy` value is `Exact` (the exact count of substitutions)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q1 (Lossy opt-in shape)"] # [doc = " The `marker` parameter implements the \"configurable replacement marker\" direction from spec"] # [doc = " §7-Q1. The default is U+FFFD (`'\\u{FFFD}'`). The final shape (whether `marker` is a parameter"] # [doc = " or a type-level default) is pending maintainer decision (spec §7-Q1)."] # [must_use] pub fn to_latin1_lossy (s : & Text , marker : char) -> Lossy < Vec < u8 > > { let marker_byte = if (marker as u32) <= 0xFF { marker as u8 } else { b'?' } ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (s . len_bytes ()) ; let mut substituted = 0usize ; for ch in s . as_str () . chars () { let cp = ch as u32 ; if cp > 0xFF { out . push (marker_byte) ; substituted += 1 ; } else { out . push (cp as u8) ; } } Lossy :: new (out , substituted , marker) }
gaps.reason: unsupported literal kind `b'?'` (only bool/int/string/float literals map)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Transcode a UTF-16 `u16` sequence to a `Text` (fallible)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (when `Ok`)"] # [doc = " When `Ok`, the result is the exact string the UTF-16 sequence encodes."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Err(TranscodeError::UnpairedSurrogate { at })` — a high surrogate at `at` is not followed"] # [doc = "   by a low surrogate (or a low surrogate appears without a preceding high surrogate)."] # [doc = " - `Err(TranscodeError::Invalid { at })` — a code unit is invalid (not expected here given"] # [doc = "   Rust's `char::decode_utf16`, but included for completeness)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never a silent U+FFFD substitution** (C1 / G2). A caller who wants U+FFFD substitution"] # [doc = " must use `from_utf16_lossy` (see FLAG below — not yet in scope in this crate)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — from_utf16_lossy"] # [doc = " The spec sketches a `from_utf16_lossy` variant (returning `Lossy<Text>`). That op is"] # [doc = " in scope per the spec surface but not yet implemented here (the operator set is complete"] # [doc = " with this strict variant; the lossy variant can be added in a follow-on without changing the"] # [doc = " strict-variant's signature). Not a blocker for this wave."] pub fn from_utf16 (units : & [u16]) -> Result < Text , TranscodeError > { let mut s = String :: with_capacity (units . len ()) ; for (at , result) in char :: decode_utf16 (units . iter () . copied ()) . enumerate () { match result { Ok (ch) => s . push (ch) , Err (_e) => { return Err (TranscodeError :: UnpairedSurrogate { at }) ; } } } Ok (Text :: from_string_unchecked (s)) }

A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use crate :: error :: { BoundaryError , EncodeError , ParseErr , TranscodeError , Utf8Error } ; # [doc = " `from_chars` round-trips: collecting chars back yields the original string."] # [test] fn from_chars_round_trips () { let cs : Vec < char > = "hello" . chars () . collect () ; let t = from_chars (& cs) ; assert_eq ! (t . as_str () , "hello") ; } # [doc = " `from_chars` on empty input yields empty Text."] # [test] fn from_chars_empty () { let t = from_chars (& []) ; assert ! (t . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " `from_chars` with multi-byte chars preserves them exactly."] # [test] fn from_chars_multibyte () { let cs : Vec < char > = "café" . chars () . collect () ; let t = from_chars (& cs) ; assert_eq ! (t . as_str () , "café") ; } # [doc = " `from_utf8` accepts valid UTF-8 bytes (round-trip)."] # [test] fn from_utf8_accepts_valid () { let bytes = "hello" . as_bytes () ; let t = from_utf8 (bytes) . expect ("valid UTF-8") ; assert_eq ! (t . as_str () , "hello") ; } # [doc = " `from_utf8` rejects invalid UTF-8 with a typed error carrying the byte index (C1 / C3)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning Ok for invalid bytes makes this fail."] # [test] fn from_utf8_rejects_invalid_with_error () { let bytes = b"\xFE\x80\x80" ; let err = from_utf8 (bytes) . expect_err ("must reject invalid UTF-8") ; match err { Utf8Error :: Invalid { byte , .. } => { assert_eq ! (byte , 0 , "error must name the byte index (C3)") ; } } } # [doc = " `from_utf8` never inserts U+FFFD silently (C1 — never-silent)."] # [test] fn from_utf8_never_silent_fffd () { let result = from_utf8 (b"\xFF") ; assert ! (result . is_err () , "invalid UTF-8 must be Err, never silent U+FFFD") ; } # [doc = " `concat` produces the concatenation of two texts."] # [test] fn concat_two_texts () { let a = Text :: new ("hello") ; let b = Text :: new (" world") ; assert_eq ! (concat (& a , & b) . as_str () , "hello world") ; } # [doc = " `concat` with empty texts is identity on the non-empty side."] # [test] fn concat_with_empty () { let a = Text :: new ("hello") ; let empty = Text :: new ("") ; assert_eq ! (concat (& a , & empty) , a) ; assert_eq ! (concat (& empty , & a) , a) ; } # [doc = " `join` inserts the separator between parts."] # [test] fn join_parts_with_separator () { let parts = vec ! [Text :: new ("a") , Text :: new ("b") , Text :: new ("c")] ; let sep = Text :: new (",") ; assert_eq ! (join (& parts , & sep) . as_str () , "a,b,c") ; } # [doc = " `join` on an empty slice returns empty Text."] # [test] fn join_empty_slice () { let t = join (& [] , & Text :: new (",")) ; assert ! (t . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " `join` on a single element returns that element (no trailing separator)."] # [test] fn join_single_element () { let parts = vec ! [Text :: new ("only")] ; let result = join (& parts , & Text :: new ("|")) ; assert_eq ! (result . as_str () , "only") ; } # [doc = " `to_upper` returns a new Text (never in-place — C4)."] # [test] fn to_upper_returns_new_text () { let s = Text :: new ("hello") ; let u = to_upper (& s) ; assert_eq ! (u . as_str () , "HELLO") ; assert_eq ! (s . as_str () , "hello" , "original must be unchanged (C4 — never in-place)") ; } # [doc = " `to_lower` returns a new Text (never in-place — C4)."] # [test] fn to_lower_returns_new_text () { let s = Text :: new ("HELLO") ; let l = to_lower (& s) ; assert_eq ! (l . as_str () , "hello") ; assert_eq ! (s . as_str () , "HELLO" , "original must be unchanged (C4)") ; } # [doc = " `trim` strips leading/trailing whitespace, returns new Text (never in-place — C4)."] # [test] fn trim_strips_whitespace_returns_new_text () { let s = Text :: new ("  hello  ") ; let t = trim (& s) ; assert_eq ! (t . as_str () , "hello") ; assert_eq ! (s . as_str () , "  hello  " , "original must be unchanged (C4)") ; } # [doc = " `trim` on text without whitespace is a no-op (identity)."] # [test] fn trim_no_whitespace_is_identity () { let s = Text :: new ("hello") ; assert_eq ! (trim (& s) . as_str () , "hello") ; } # [doc = " `replace` replaces occurrences, returns new Text (never in-place — C4)."] # [test] fn replace_returns_new_text () { let s = Text :: new ("aababc") ; let from = Text :: new ("ab") ; let to = Text :: new ("X") ; let r = replace (& s , & from , & to) ; assert_eq ! (r . as_str () , "aXXc") ; assert_eq ! (s . as_str () , "aababc" , "original must be unchanged (C4)") ; } # [doc = " `replace` with empty `from` matches Rust's `str::replace` semantics exactly."] # [test] fn replace_empty_from_inserts_between_chars () { let s = Text :: new ("hello") ; let empty = Text :: new ("") ; let r = replace (& s , & empty , & Text :: new ("X")) ; assert_eq ! (r . as_str () , "XhXeXlXlXoX") ; assert_eq ! (s . as_str () , "hello" , "original must be unchanged (C4)") ; } # [doc = " `len_bytes` equals the number of raw bytes."] # [test] fn len_bytes_counts_raw_bytes () { let s = Text :: new ("café") ; assert_eq ! (len_bytes (& s) , 5) ; } # [doc = " `len_chars` counts Unicode codepoints, not bytes."] # [test] fn len_chars_counts_codepoints () { let s = Text :: new ("café") ; assert_eq ! (len_chars (& s) , 4) ; } # [doc = " `len_bytes` == `len_chars` for pure ASCII."] # [test] fn len_bytes_equals_len_chars_for_ascii () { let s = Text :: new ("hello") ; assert_eq ! (len_bytes (& s) , len_chars (& s)) ; } # [doc = " `chars` returns the codepoints in order."] # [test] fn chars_returns_codepoints_in_order () { let s = Text :: new ("abc") ; let cs = chars (& s) ; assert_eq ! (cs , vec ! ['a' , 'b' , 'c']) ; } # [doc = " `chars` on empty returns empty."] # [test] fn chars_empty () { let cs = chars (& Text :: new ("")) ; assert ! (cs . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " `slice` on a valid range returns the exact substring."] # [test] fn slice_valid_range_returns_exact () { let s = Text :: new ("hello world") ; let sub = slice (& s , 6 , 11) . expect ("valid slice") ; assert_eq ! (sub . as_str () , "world") ; } # [doc = " `slice` with start == end returns empty Text."] # [test] fn slice_empty_range () { let s = Text :: new ("hello") ; let sub = slice (& s , 2 , 2) . expect ("valid empty slice") ; assert ! (sub . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " `slice` with out-of-range end returns Err(OutOfRange) — never a silent truncation (C1)."] # [test] fn slice_out_of_range_is_err_never_silent_truncation () { let s = Text :: new ("hi") ; let err = slice (& s , 0 , 100) . expect_err ("must be Err") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { .. }) , "out-of-range must be Err(OutOfRange), not silent truncation (C1)") ; } # [doc = " `slice` with an inverted range (`start > end`) returns Err(InvalidRange) — never a panic."] # [test] fn slice_inverted_range_is_err_never_panic () { let s = Text :: new ("hello world") ; let err = slice (& s , 5 , 3) . expect_err ("inverted range must be Err") ; assert_eq ! (err , BoundaryError :: InvalidRange { start : 5 , end : 3 } , "inverted range must be Err(InvalidRange), never a slice-index panic (C1)") ; } # [doc = " `slice` on a non-char-boundary byte index returns Err(NotCharBoundary) (C1)."] # [test] fn slice_non_char_boundary_is_err () { let s = Text :: new ("café") ; let err = slice (& s , 0 , 4) . expect_err ("must be Err for non-char-boundary end") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , BoundaryError :: NotCharBoundary { byte : 4 }) , "must report the offending byte (C3): got {:?}" , err) ; } # [doc = " `char_at` returns the correct char for a valid index."] # [test] fn char_at_valid_index_returns_char () { let s = Text :: new ("hello") ; assert_eq ! (char_at (& s , 0) . expect ("valid") , 'h') ; assert_eq ! (char_at (& s , 4) . expect ("valid") , 'o') ; } # [doc = " `char_at` with an out-of-range index returns Err(OutOfRange) — never a sentinel (C1)."] # [test] fn char_at_out_of_range_is_err_never_sentinel () { let s = Text :: new ("hi") ; let err = char_at (& s , 100) . expect_err ("must be Err") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { .. }) , "out-of-range char_at must be Err, not a sentinel char (C1)") ; } # [doc = " `parse_int` parses a valid decimal integer."] # [test] fn parse_int_valid_decimal () { assert_eq ! (parse_int (& Text :: new ("42")) . expect ("valid") , 42) ; assert_eq ! (parse_int (& Text :: new ("-7")) . expect ("valid") , - 7) ; assert_eq ! (parse_int (& Text :: new ("0")) . expect ("valid") , 0) ; } # [doc = " `parse_int` rejects empty input with Err(Empty) — never a sentinel 0 (C1)."] # [test] fn parse_int_empty_is_err_never_sentinel () { let err = parse_int (& Text :: new ("")) . expect_err ("empty must be Err") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , ParseErr :: Empty) , "empty input must be ParseErr::Empty, not a sentinel 0 (C1)") ; } # [doc = " `parse_int` rejects non-numeric input with Err(Invalid) carrying the byte index (C3)."] # [test] fn parse_int_invalid_carries_index_and_context () { let err = parse_int (& Text :: new ("12abc")) . expect_err ("non-numeric must be Err") ; match err { ParseErr :: Invalid { at , .. } => { assert_eq ! (at , 2 , "error must name byte index 2 (where 'a' is)") ; } other => panic ! ("expected Invalid, got {other:?}") , } } # [doc = " `parse_bool` parses \"true\" and \"false\" exactly."] # [test] fn parse_bool_valid () { assert ! (parse_bool (& Text :: new ("true")) . expect ("valid")) ; assert ! (! parse_bool (& Text :: new ("false")) . expect ("valid")) ; } # [doc = " `parse_bool` rejects empty input with Err(Empty) — never a sentinel false (C1)."] # [test] fn parse_bool_empty_is_err_never_sentinel () { let err = parse_bool (& Text :: new ("")) . expect_err ("empty must be Err") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , ParseErr :: Empty) , "empty input must be ParseErr::Empty, not sentinel false (C1)") ; } # [doc = " `parse_bool` rejects unrecognized strings with Err(Invalid) (C1)."] # [test] fn parse_bool_invalid_is_err () { let err = parse_bool (& Text :: new ("yes")) . expect_err ("unrecognized must be Err") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , ParseErr :: Invalid { .. }) , "must be ParseErr::Invalid") ; } # [doc = " `parse_bool` is case-sensitive: \"True\" is not accepted (C1 — no silent coercion)."] # [test] fn parse_bool_is_case_sensitive_no_silent_coercion () { assert ! (parse_bool (& Text :: new ("True")) . is_err () , "parse_bool must be case-sensitive (no silent coercion — C1)") ; } # [doc = " `encode_utf8` round-trips: bytes → Text → bytes."] # [test] fn encode_utf8_round_trips () { let t = Text :: new ("hello") ; let bytes = encode_utf8 (& t) ; let t2 = from_utf8 (& bytes) . expect ("valid UTF-8") ; assert_eq ! (t , t2 , "encode_utf8 → from_utf8 must round-trip") ; } # [doc = " `to_utf16` + `from_utf16` round-trip."] # [test] fn to_utf16_from_utf16_round_trip () { let t = Text :: new ("hello 🌍") ; let units = to_utf16 (& t) ; let t2 = from_utf16 (& units) . expect ("valid UTF-16") ; assert_eq ! (t , t2 , "UTF-8 → UTF-16 → UTF-8 must round-trip") ; } # [doc = " `to_utf16` + `from_utf16` round-trip for ASCII."] # [test] fn to_utf16_from_utf16_round_trip_ascii () { let t = Text :: new ("abc") ; let t2 = from_utf16 (& to_utf16 (& t)) . expect ("valid") ; assert_eq ! (t , t2) ; } # [doc = " `to_latin1` succeeds for ASCII-range characters."] # [test] fn to_latin1_succeeds_for_latin1_chars () { let t = Text :: new ("hello") ; let bytes = to_latin1 (& t) . expect ("ASCII is Latin-1") ; assert_eq ! (bytes , b"hello") ; } # [doc = " `to_latin1` succeeds for Latin-1-range (U+0000–U+00FF) characters."] # [test] fn to_latin1_succeeds_for_full_latin1_range () { let t = Text :: new ("cañón") ; let bytes = to_latin1 (& t) . expect ("all chars are Latin-1") ; assert_eq ! (bytes [0] , b'c') ; } # [doc = " `to_latin1` rejects non-Latin-1 chars with Err carrying the char and index (C1 / C3)."] # [test] fn to_latin1_rejects_non_latin1_with_error () { let t = Text :: new ("price: €") ; let err = to_latin1 (& t) . expect_err ("€ is not Latin-1") ; match err { EncodeError :: Unrepresentable { ch , at , target_encoding , } => { assert_eq ! (ch , '€' , "error must name the offending char (C3)") ; assert_eq ! (at , 7 , "error must name the char index (C3)") ; assert_eq ! (target_encoding , "Latin-1" , "error must name the target encoding (C3)") ; } } } # [doc = " `to_latin1` never inserts U+FFFD silently (C1 — never-silent)."] # [test] fn to_latin1_never_silent_fffd () { let t = Text :: new ("€") ; assert ! (to_latin1 (& t) . is_err () , "non-Latin-1 char must be Err, never silent U+FFFD (C1)") ; } # [doc = " `to_latin1_lossy` substitutes non-Latin-1 chars and counts them (C1 opt-in lossiness)."] # [test] fn to_latin1_lossy_substitutes_and_counts () { let t = Text :: new ("hello €") ; let result = to_latin1_lossy (& t , '?') ; assert_eq ! (result . substituted , 1 , "exactly one char substituted") ; assert_eq ! (result . marker , '?') ; assert_eq ! (result . value . last () , Some (& b'?')) ; } # [doc = " `to_latin1_lossy` with all-Latin-1 input has zero substitutions (lossless case)."] # [test] fn to_latin1_lossy_zero_substitutions_for_latin1 () { let t = Text :: new ("hello") ; let result = to_latin1_lossy (& t , '\u{FFFD}') ; assert_eq ! (result . substituted , 0 , "no substitutions for Latin-1-only input") ; assert ! (result . is_lossless ()) ; } # [doc = " `to_latin1_lossy` substitution count is un-droppable (G2 / C1 — the lossiness is in the"] # [doc = " type; a caller cannot receive lossy output without seeing the count)."] # [test] fn to_latin1_lossy_count_is_in_type_not_hidden () { let t = Text :: new ("€€€") ; let r = to_latin1_lossy (& t , '?') ; assert_eq ! (r . substituted , 3 , "substitution count must be in the Lossy value (G2 / C1)") ; } # [doc = " `from_utf16` rejects an unpaired surrogate with Err(UnpairedSurrogate) (C1)."] # [test] fn from_utf16_rejects_unpaired_surrogate () { let units : Vec < u16 > = vec ! [0xD800 , 0x0041] ; let err = from_utf16 (& units) . expect_err ("unpaired surrogate must be Err") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , TranscodeError :: UnpairedSurrogate { at : 0 }) , "error must be UnpairedSurrogate at index 0 (C3): got {:?}" , err) ; } # [doc = " `from_utf16` accepts a valid BMP string."] # [test] fn from_utf16_accepts_valid_bmp () { let units : Vec < u16 > = "hello" . encode_utf16 () . collect () ; let t = from_utf16 (& units) . expect ("valid UTF-16") ; assert_eq ! (t . as_str () , "hello") ; } # [doc = " UTF-8 round-trip: `from_utf8(encode_utf8(t)) == t` for all `Text`."] # [doc = " Tested over a representative set of strings."] # [test] fn property_utf8_round_trip_encode_decode () { let samples = ["hello" , "café" , "日本語" , "emoji 🌍" , "" , "\0" , "ASCII only" ,] ; for s in & samples { let t = Text :: new (s) ; let bytes = encode_utf8 (& t) ; let back = from_utf8 (& bytes) . unwrap_or_else (| e | panic ! ("round-trip failed for {:?}: {e}" , s)) ; assert_eq ! (t , back , "UTF-8 round-trip must be identity for {:?}" , s) ; } } # [doc = " `from_utf8 → encode_utf8` round-trip: `encode_utf8(from_utf8(b).unwrap()) == b`"] # [doc = " for all valid UTF-8 byte slices."] # [test] fn property_utf8_round_trip_decode_encode () { let samples : & [& [u8]] = & [b"hello" , "café" . as_bytes () , "日本語" . as_bytes ()] ; for & bytes in samples { let t = from_utf8 (bytes) . unwrap_or_else (| e | panic ! ("valid UTF-8 failed: {e}")) ; let back = encode_utf8 (& t) ; assert_eq ! (back , bytes , "decode → encode round-trip must be identity") ; } } # [doc = " `parse_int` round-trip: parsing the string representation of an i64 yields the original."] # [test] fn property_parse_int_round_trips_for_sample_values () { let values : & [i64] = & [0 , 1 , - 1 , 42 , - 42 , i64 :: MAX , i64 :: MIN , 100 , - 100 , 9999] ; for & v in values { let s = v . to_string () ; let t = Text :: new (& s) ; let parsed = parse_int (& t) . unwrap_or_else (| e | panic ! ("parse_int({v}) failed: {e}")) ; assert_eq ! (parsed , v , "parse_int round-trip failed for {v}") ; } } # [doc = " `parse_bool` round-trip: the bool → string → bool identity holds."] # [test] fn property_parse_bool_round_trips () { for & b in & [true , false] { let s = b . to_string () ; let t = Text :: new (& s) ; let parsed = parse_bool (& t) . unwrap_or_else (| e | panic ! ("parse_bool({b}) failed: {e}")) ; assert_eq ! (parsed , b , "parse_bool round-trip failed for {b}") ; } } # [doc = " `to_utf16` → `from_utf16` round-trip (property: identity for all samples)."] # [test] fn property_utf16_round_trip () { let samples = ["hello" , "café" , "日本語" , "emoji 🌍" , "" , "mixed ASCII and Unicode: αβγ" ,] ; for s in & samples { let t = Text :: new (s) ; let units = to_utf16 (& t) ; let back = from_utf16 (& units) . unwrap_or_else (| e | panic ! ("UTF-16 round-trip failed for {:?}: {e}" , s)) ; assert_eq ! (t , back , "UTF-16 round-trip must be identity for {:?}" , s) ; } } # [doc = " `slice` identity: slicing the entire string returns the original (Exact, no truncation)."] # [test] fn slice_full_range_is_identity () { let s = Text :: new ("hello") ; let sub = slice (& s , 0 , s . len_bytes ()) . expect ("full range is valid") ; assert_eq ! (sub , s , "slice of full range must equal the original") ; } # [doc = " `chars(from_chars(cs)) == cs` — chars round-trip (property: identity for any char vec)."] # [test] fn property_chars_round_trip () { let samples : & [& str] = & ["hello" , "café" , "αβγ" , "🌍" , ""] ; for & s in samples { let cs : Vec < char > = s . chars () . collect () ; let t = from_chars (& cs) ; let back : Vec < char > = chars (& t) ; assert_eq ! (back , cs , "chars round-trip must be identity for {:?}" , s) ; } } }
gaps.reason: #[cfg(test)] item — out of scope for this PoC's transpilation surface (excluded from the expressible-fraction denominator, but recorded, never silently skipped)
